rulings.Case Title: SANDRA WALKER VS. THE KROGER CO., A FOREIGN CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One **Filed Under Seal**
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 29, 2025, line 2, 2 - PLAINTIFF SANDRA WALKER'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One **Filed Under Seal** Off calendar in light of the pending stipulation to stay the case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.

rulings.Case Title: LUIS E. MORELL VS. VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Disovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 29, 2025, line 3 PLAINTIFF LUIS MORELL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One Opposing party Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. has not provided courtesy copies as required by SFLR 2.7B. The motion is continued to February 20, 2025. The opposing party shall immediately provide the courtesy copies with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The court will consider sanctions if opposing party fails to follow the local rule. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)

rulings.Case Title: WUWTA, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. DR. MEHDI MATIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 29, 2025, line 1, DEFENDANTS DR. MEHDI MATIN , AN INDIVIDUAL, M. MATIN SUGICAL AND DENTAL GROUP INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT This case arises from a contract between M. Matin Surgical and Dental Group ("Matin Group") and WUWTA, Inc. for marketing services. Defendant Mehdi Matin is the principal of Matin Group. Matin and Matin Group have filed this special motion to strike the complaint as a SLAPP. (CCP 425.16.) Defendant Mina Katchooi is not a party to the motion. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
